llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Only one AI arm available: genai_condition with GPT-4o-powered chatbot. This is compared against control_condition with no technological assistance.
llm_extracted.notes: Primary outcome is task efficiency measured in seconds (time to complete fraud risk assessment). Two subgroups reported: novice and experienced auditors. Using novice auditor data as primary comparison since it shows statistically significant effect (p=0.01) and is reported first. The paper states novices were '23 percent faster' with GenAI. Sample sizes (n) are not provided in the arms array. Standard deviations/standard errors are not reported, only means and t-statistics. The experienced auditor comparison shows similar pattern but non-significant at conventional alpha (p=0.09 one-tailed).
source_quotes: Novice auditors with GenAI completed the task in 595.15 seconds, on average, versus 769.46 seconds without GenAI (t = 2.51, p = 0.01), indicating a significant efficiency improvement for novices.
Task Efficiency, equal to the amount of time in seconds participants spent completing the fraud risk assessment task.
